text.title: EVM Step-by-Step Process
text.summary: Four steps to implement EVM: establish the time-phased baseline, measure progress with objective earning rules, compute indices deterministically, and forecast completion with multiple scenarios.

rule.statement: Build the PV curve by distributing BAC across the project timeline per the WBS work packages; the curve must reflect actual planned expenditure, not a straight line.
rule.rationale: A linear PV curve produces misleading SV/SPI in phases with front-loaded or back-loaded work; the S-curve shape reflects real resource allocation and gives accurate schedule performance signals.

list.item: Define BAC (total approved budget) per work package and in total.
Assign an earning rule to each work package: 0/100 (short tasks), 50/50 (multi-period tasks), milestone-weighted, or percent-complete (long tasks with clear DoD).
Build the time-phased PV curve by distributing work-package budgets across planned periods.
Lock the baseline in version control; tag as baseline-v1.

rule.statement: Post an EV event only when the work package passes its acceptance criterion (Definition of Done); partial credit on deliverables that are not yet accepted is forbidden.
rule.rationale: EV posted before DoD acceptance inflates performance indices and hides real schedule risk; unaccepted deliverables often require rework that consumes additional AC without new EV.

list.item: At each period end: assess actual completion status for each work package per its earning rule.
Calculate EV = sum of (work-package budget x earned fraction) for all packages.
Record AC from the accounting system; never estimate AC from invoices alone.
Compute SV, CV, SPI, CPI from the period's PV, EV, AC.

section.title: Step 3: Compute and Report

rule.statement: Force all EVM computation through a deterministic script or tool; the LLM narrates and contextualizes the output but does not compute the numbers.
rule.rationale: LLMs round inconsistently, conflate SV (currency) with schedule slip (time), and introduce GREEN-washing when prompted to summarize. Deterministic computation eliminates these errors at source.

section.reference: Deterministic EVM index calculator from YAML inputs
EVM dashboard template with RAG status

rule.statement: Rebaseline only when a formally approved scope change alters the project; never rebaseline to hide variance — preserve the original baseline alongside the new one for audit.
rule.rationale: Rebaselining to make RAG green is EVM fraud; it destroys the performance history and makes future forecasts meaningless. Sponsors and auditors must see the original vs. revised baseline side by side.

antipattern.description: Rebaselining monthly to maintain a "green" dashboard after each rough period.
antipattern.reason: Each rebaseline resets the indices to 1.0, erasing the evidence of chronic underperformance. Cumulative drift becomes invisible, and the final overrun surprises everyone.
